_comment: Add or remove models here — they appear in the model dropdown. provider: 'bedrock' (uses the connected AWS Bedrock account), 'openai' (OpenAI API), or 'anthropic' (Anthropic API). For openai/anthropic set keyEnv to the environment variable holding the API key (e.g. OPENAI_API_KEY); the model shows as available only when that key is present. 'vision' true enables image uploads for that model.
_maxOutputComment: maxOutput is the model's OWN hard output ceiling, in tokens — not a budget we impose. Generation asks for this much and never less, so the only limit on a document is what the model itself can emit. Getting it WRONG IN EITHER DIRECTION is visible: too low silently truncates long output, too high makes Bedrock reject every call with a validation error. Verify against the provider's model card before editing.

models.name: Claude Sonnet 4.6
models.modelComment: set to us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-5 once AWS clears it for this account

models.name: Nova 2 Lite
models.maxOutputComment: UNVERIFIED — conservative placeholder. Prior-gen Nova Lite capped at 5k output; raise once the Nova 2 model card is checked.

models.name: Nemotron 3 Super 120B
models.modelComment: bare id — no us. inference profile needed; text-only (the 120B is not the VL variant)
models.maxOutputComment: Bedrock accepts up to >=65536 for this model (probed 2026-07-16, all of 8k/16k/32k/64k returned end_turn). 32768 chosen as a safe working ceiling — comfortably above the 24k office budget, so 30-slide decks no longer truncate.
